label: qwen36_35b_a3b_gguf_q4ks_w7900_readme_sweep

hardware.note: W7900 (gfx1100) rerun of GGUF UD-Q4_K_S sweep so README Performance tables can include a GGUF column alongside packed PARO and llama.cpp Q4_K_M baselines.

fastpath.effective_use_wmma_prefill_all: true at every measured run for every shape
fastpath.effective_use_gemv_decode_all: true at every measured run for every shape

common_benchmark_command: HIPENGINE_GGUF_AOTRITON_PREFILL=v3 HIPENGINE_GGUF_ALLOW_UNSAFE_QWEN35MOE_FASTPATHS=1 HIPENGINE_GGUF_DECODE_REPACK=1 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt PYTHONPATH=. python scripts/qwen35_gguf_bench.py --model /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_S.gguf --quant gguf_q4_k_s --prompt-length <N> --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 1 --warmup-runs 1 --measured-runs 3 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --use-gemv-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --json <raw-json>

correctness_and_dispatch_gate.note: Reuses the dual-column gate from benchmarks/results/2026-05-22-hipengine-qwen36-35b-a3b-q4km-q4ks-final-gate-4k128-accepted.json (167 passed). No source change introduced in this artifact; runs at the same retained state.

load_timing_comparison.note: GGUF persistent decode-repack layout transform makes load_seconds materially higher than PARO safetensors mmap. Numbers below are medians of load_seconds across all sessions (1 warmup + 3 measured) per shape.

notes: Each shape was measured with 1 warmup run + 3 measured runs of qwen35_gguf_bench.py.
Auto-tune chunked prefill resolves to linear=1024 / moe=1024 / full_attn_query=4096 / full_attn_post=1024 / full_attn_rope=1024 for all shapes above 1K, matching the PARO long-context policy.
128K/128 tracked allocator peak (25.108 GiB) exceeds the 24 GiB consumer-card budget; this is expected and is why the README status flags Q4_K_S long-context as W7900/48 GiB territory.

rocm-smi reported a low-power idle state (~14 W) between sessions on this W7900 host; W7900 prefill numbers are noticeably below the same kernels measured on the local RX 7900 XTX in the dual-column final-gate (e.g. 4K Q4_K_S prefill 1737 vs 2722 tok/s). Decode numbers are within 1-3% of the RX 7900 XTX measurement.

Load timing: GGUF UD-Q4_K_S median load_seconds per session is roughly 60s on W7900, vs ~24s for PARO packed sessions on the same hardware. The dominant cost is the persistent decode-repack pass that produces T16 tiles for the Q4/Q5/Q6/Q8 GEMV decode kernels.
